atlases.title: Pathogen Atlas
atlases.summary: A curated origin-and-spread atlas that links geography, uncertainty, papers, and prior writing instead of flattening pathogens into another story stream.
atlases.evidence_model: Curated origin claims, route confidence, and linked papers
atlases.long_note: The pathogen atlas now runs as a dedicated dark atlas app, using the same product class as the maritime build while still drawing from the curated epi-dossier atlas export.

atlases.title: Maritime Disease Atlas
atlases.summary: A darker, map-forward atlas of shipboard disease ecology, ports, vectors, and the movement constraints of life at sea.
atlases.evidence_model: Curated map scenarios with route, mechanism, and historical narrative
atlases.long_note: This is the main visual precedent for the atlas family and stays closer to a control-room cartographic interface than a standard article page.

atlases.title: Revolutionary War Disease Atlas
atlases.summary: A historical epidemiology section focused on colonial and Revolutionary-era disease ecology, troop movement, and the public-health constraints around the founding period.
atlases.evidence_model: Essay-linked historical synthesis with future map promotion
atlases.long_note: This section launches as a structured historical atlas page with linked essays first, then graduates into a fuller interactive once the map layer is ready.

atlases.title: Viking Health Atlas
atlases.summary: A Norse-world disease and health map that ties Greenland, Vinland, settlement ecology, and pre-Columbian contact arguments into one visual surface.
atlases.evidence_model: Prototype map plus linked historical essays
atlases.long_note: The current Viking build is strong enough to surface publicly as a prototype atlas while the surrounding essay and evidence layer fills in.
